obituaries.name: Charlene Heitmiller
obituaries.summary: Charlene Heitmiller January 4, 1933 — January 26, 2026 Charlene Annie Heitmiller, age 93, of West, passed away late Monday morning, January 26, 2026 at Baylor Scott & White Hillcrest Hospital in Waco. Funeral Services will be held 11:00 a.m. Saturday, February 7, 2026 at Aderhold Funeral Home Chapel in West, with Pastor Jeff Richter officiating. Burial will follow at Closner Cemetery. The family will receive visitors from 5:00 to 7:00 p.m. Friday, February 6, 2026 at the funeral home. Charlene was born on January 4, 1933, in Gerald, the beloved daughter of Hugo and Emma (Bode) Otto. A proud 1950 graduate of West High School, Charlene's life was a testament to love, resilience, and dedication

obituaries.name: Danny Goates
obituaries.summary: Danny Goates November 28, 1938 — February 12, 2026 Danny Lee Goates, age 87, of Gholson passed away late Thursday evening, February 12, 2026 at St. Anthony's Care Center in Waco. Funeral services will be held 10:00 a.m. Friday, February 20, 2026 at Aderhold Funeral Home Chapel in West, with Deacon Robin Waters officiating. Masonic Graveside Rites will follow at Oakwood Cemetery in Waco. The family will receive visitors from 6:00 to 8:00 p.m. Thursday at the funeral home. Born on November 28, 1938, in Fort Worth, Danny was the beloved son of Clara Mae (Dollins) and William Riley Goates Jr. He spent his early years in the heart of Texas, graduating from West High School in 1957. obituaries.name: Ed Traywick Jr.
obituaries.summary: Ed Traywick Jr. March 9, 1946 — February 9, 2026 Edward A. "Ed" Traywick Jr, age 79, of Waco, passed away Monday morning, February 9, 2026 at Baylor Scott & White Hillcrest Hospital in Waco. A rosary and Mass of Christian Burial celebrated 10:00 a.m. Monday at St. Mary's Catholic Church of the Assumption in West, with Rev. Ed Karasek as celebrant. Military Graveside Rites will follow at St. Mary's Cemetery, conducted by the West Veterans Honor Guard. The family will receive visitors from 4:00 to 6:00 p.m. Sunday at Aderhold Funeral Home. Ed was born on March 9, 1946, in Waco, to Lillian (Hanzelka) and Edward Traywick Sr. A lifelong Waco-area resident, Ed attended St. Mary's, St. Joseph's, an

obituaries.name: Emilia Moreno
obituaries.summary: Emilia Moreno August 8, 1927 — February 15, 2026 Emilia Moreno, age 98, of Penelope, passed away Sunday afternoon, February 15, 2026 at her residence. A rosary will be recited 6:00 p.m. Wednesday at Aderhold Funeral Home Chapel in West, followed by visitation until 8:00 p.m. Mass of Christian Burial will be celebrated 10:00 a.m. Thursday, February 19, 2026 at Church of the Nativity of the Blessed Virgin Mary in Penelope, with Rev. Thomas Jones as celebrant. Burial will follow at St. Joseph's Cemetery near Penelope. Emilia was born August 8, 1927, in Villa de Arista, San Luis Potosí, Mexico, the daughter of Jose and Maria (Gonzalez) Mendoza. On March 19, 1945, she began a beautiful journey wi

obituaries.name: Gloria Bollier
obituaries.summary: Gloria Bollier September 9, 1941 — February 4, 2026 Gloria Sue Elliott Bollier, age 85, of Gholson, Texas, passed away Wednesday morning, February 4, 2026. Graveside services will be held 10:00 a.m. Friday, February 6, 2026 at Gholson Cemetery. Gloria was born on September 9, 1941, in Tyler, Texas. She was preceded in death by her parents, Walter J. Elliott, former Sergeant Major with the Texas Highway Patrol, and Gladys Elliott Estes. Gloria's special bond with her dad was present even in her death. She married the love of her life, James Lillard Bollier, on June 6,1982, and they were married for 20 years. Jim was her person, and his passing left an unfilled void in her life. Together they

obituaries.name: Helen Jo Hurst
obituaries.summary: Helen Jo Hurst June 24, 1943 — February 21, 2026 Helen Jo Hurst, 82, of Axtell, Texas, passed away peacefully on February 21, 2026 at her residence. Funeral Service will be held 10:00 a.m. Friday, February 27, 2026 at Aderhold Funeral Home Chapel in West, with Eddie Scheler officiating. Graveside Service will be held 11:30 a.m. Friday at Axtell Cemetery. The family will receive visitors from 5:00 to 7:00 p.m. Thursday at the funeral home. Born in West, Texas, on June 24, 1943, Helen dedicated much of her life to caring for others as a Licensed Vocational Nurse (LVN) at many offices including Groesbeck State Detention Center, Meridian Hospital and Clinic, and Hill Regional Hospital. She spent

obituaries.name: Joan Kasik
obituaries.summary: Joan Kasik December 20, 1951 — February 6, 2026 Karen Joan (Bordovsky) Kasik, age 74, of Waco, passed away Friday evening, February 6, 2026 at Ascension Providence Hospital in Waco. A rosary will be recited 6:00 p.m. Friday, February 13, 2026 at Aderhold Funeral Home Chapel in West, followed by visitation until 8:00 p.m. Mass of Christian Burial will be celebrated 10:00 a.m. Saturday, February 14, 2026 at St. Joseph Catholic Church in Bellmead, with Rev. Fernando Saenz as celebrant. Burial will follow at West Brethren Cemetery. Joan was born on December 20, 1951, in Mart, the daughter of Billy and Rosalie (Pechacek) Bordovsky. She graduated from Mart High School in 1970 and earned her Bachel

obituaries.name: Kathy Hooten
obituaries.summary: Kathy Hooten July 27, 1946 — February 9, 2026 Mary Kathleen "Kathy" Hooten, age 79 of Waco, joined the arms of the Lord on Monday, February 09, 2026 at Avir in Waco. Memorial Service will be held 2:00 p.m. Monday, February 16, 2026 at Aderhold Funeral Home Chapel in West, with Rex Bland Officiating. Burial will follow at Gholson Cemetery. The live stream of the service can be viewed HERE . Kathy was born in Houston, Texas on July 27, 1946, to the late Kelly and Helen Donaldson. The third of the six "Donaldson girls," Kathy fondly remembered her childhood adventures with her sisters and delighted others with stories of growing up in the small town of Gholson, Texas. She graduated from West Hi

obituaries.name: Livy Etter
obituaries.summary: Livy Etter May 8, 1932 — January 30, 2026 Livy Barnes "L.B." Etter, age 93, of Abbott, passed away Friday evening, January 30, 2026 at his residence. Graveside Service will be held 10:00 a.m. Thursday, February 5, 2026 at Hillcrest Garden of Memory in Hillsboro, with Pastor Jeremy Begges officiating. The family will receive visitors from 6:00 to 8:00 p.m. Wednesday at Aderhold Funeral Home in West. Livy was born on May 8, 1932, in Dawson, the son of George and Erma (Woodlee) Etter. He attended school in Aquilla and Abbott. On September 23, 1955, he was united in marriage to Pauline Morgan in Waco. Pauline preceded him in death on November 7, 2024. Livy was a faithful member of Lebanon Church

obituaries.name: Wilburn Willis
obituaries.summary: Wilburn Willis February 1, 1939 — February 7, 2026 Wilburn Lee Willis, age 87, of Waco, passed away Saturday morning, February 7, 2026 at the Atrium of Bellmead. Funeral Service will be held 10:30 a.m. Friday, February 13, 2026 at West Brethren Church, with Bro. Terry Crawford officiating. Military Graveside Rites will follow at West Brethren Cemetery near West, conducted by West Veterans Honor Guard. The family will receive visitors from 5:00 to 6:30 p.m. Thursday at Aderhold Funeral Home Chapel. Wilburn was born on February 1, 1939, in Waco, Texas, the son of the late Callie (Carter) and Cecil Willis. He was a 1957 graduate of La Vega High School and later earned a degree in law enforcemen

After graduating from Bellaire High School in Houston, Texas, where she was the Reserve Officers’ Training Corps’ (ROTC) elected representative, Kay attended Baylor University, where she met her future husband, William Bondar Jr. From this union, she welcomed her only child, Nancy Lee. He weighed 6 pounds 15 ounces and was 19 inches long. His family is incredibly thankful to have been blessed with almost 4 hours with him before he went to his heavenly home. obituaries.name: Ann Hough Obituary February 22, 2026 - Grace Gardens Funeral Home & Crematorium
obituaries.summary: Ann is survived by her husband of 28 years, Michael Hough, 4 daughters Jennifer Washburn, Shawna Orlandos, Elise Baker, Julie Hough and 3 grandchildren as well as her brother Bill Swift. Ann and Mike are residents of Woodway Texas having retired in early 2023 moving here from California where Ann was an Auditor. Ann was very loved and known for her smile and servant’s heart. Ann and Mike are members of Crossroads Fellowship Church. They had adopted Baylor University and could often be found at various sporting events. Ann was active in various ministries that focus on feeding the homeless and ministering to female inmates. For the last two years she served as treasurer for MCRW stepping down

obituaries.name: Bill Spivey
obituaries.summary: Bill Spivey December 19, 1949 — February 15, 2026 Listen to Obituary Bill Spivey of Lorena, Texas, fondly known as “Uncle Bill” – and lovingly called Papa by his grandchildren – passed away peacefully surrounded by family the evening of Sunday, February 15, 2026, in Waco, Texas, at the age of 76. Born on December 19, 1949, in Waco, Texas, Bill was and will forever be cherished and be a significant part of all the lives who knew him. He lived a devoted life to his family and close friends and was willing to help anyone in need. Bill was a hard worker, determined to provide for his family to give them a better life than he had. A loving brother, uncle, husband, father, grandfather, friend, lea

obituaries.name: Gary Dale Taylor Obituary February 18, 2026 - Grace Gardens Funeral Home & Crematorium
obituaries.summary: Gary Dale Taylor of Sinton, Texas, passed away peacefully, surrounded by his beloved children and grandchildren on February 18, 2026, at the age of 70 in Waco, Texas. He was a cherished father, a grandfather to 11 grandchildren, and friend to many throughout his life.

obituaries.name: Gayle Berotte Obituary February 2, 2026 - Grace Gardens Funeral Home & Crematorium
obituaries.summary: Gayle Berotte, 71, of Waco, Texas, peacefully entered eternal rest on Monday, February 2, 2026. She was born on July 16, 1954, in Los Angeles, California, to the late Jay D. Somerville and Mattie Lee Somerville, who both preceded her in death.

obituaries.name: Nancy Melinda "Mendy" Hamilton Obituary February 16, 2026 - Grace Gardens Funeral Home & Crematorium
obituaries.summary: Nancy Melinda "Mendy" Hamilton entered the comfort of her Savior Jesus Christ on February 16th, 2026, in Waco, Texas. We invite all of her friends and family to honor her life with us at 2 PM on Saturday, February 28th, 2026, at the Grace Gardens Funeral Home in Woodway, Texas. The service will be followed there by two hours of come-and-go viewing and supportive rejoicing. Our beloved Dr. Rex Bland, pastor of Olive Branch Baptist Church of Axtell, Texas, will lead the funeral service, and our dear friend Mr. Charlie Burch will bless us with both solo and congregational songs of hope and worship.

obituaries.name: Patricia Ann Schwarz
obituaries.summary: Patricia Ann Schwarz February 13, 1948 — February 22, 2026 Waco, Texas Listen to Obituary Patricia Ann McGee Schwarz passed away peacefully at home in Waco, Texas on Sunday, February 22, 2026. She was born on February 13, 1948, in Ponca City, Oklahoma, to Robert Vernon McGee and Margie Ruth Holloway McGee. She graduated from Sulphur High School in Louisiana, and completed a Bachelor of Education from McNeese State University in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. She taught school for 28 years including a long tenure at Kendrick Elementary School in Waco. She is survived by her husband of forty-three years, Steven Wayne Schwarz of Waco; son Steven Robert Schwarz and wife Kate and grandchildren Riley and

obituaries.name: Ralph Carpenter
obituaries.summary: Ralph Carpenter June 29, 1956 — February 15, 2026 Listen to Obituary Ralph Edward Carpenter passed away on February 15, 2026, at Baylor Scott & White Medical Center in Waco. Ralph was born on June 29, 1956, in Waco, Texas, to Frank Carpenter and Rose Blackwell Carpenter. A lifelong member of the Waco community, he graduated from University High School in 1975. He dedicated his professional life to serving others through his work as an office manager at Ascension Providence Hospital, where he faithfully served for 35 years. Ralph was a man of faith and was a member of both Emanuel Baptist Church and Downsville Baptist Church. He was preceded in death by his parents. He is lovingly survived by

obituaries.name: Traci Alisha Fischer Obituary February 20, 2026 - Grace Gardens Funeral Home & Crematorium
obituaries.summary: Traci Fischer (Hill) went home to be with her Lord and Savior Jesus on 20 Feb 2026 after being ill for several years. She was born on 26 Sep 1970 in Greenwood, MS to Charles and Terry Hill. She excelled in sports and survived a devastating softball injury in 1982. She's a proud alumnus of Mississippi State University, was a member of the Phi Mu Sorority and graduated with a Bachelor's Degree in Communications. She met her husband Paul through Harmony met each other for the first time and watched the Mississippi Riverkings hockey team and eventually would attend many more games together. Traci is survived by her mother Terry Hill, preceded in death by her father Charles Hill, her older sister

obituaries.name: Carol Kolb
obituaries.summary: Carol Mae Riley Kolb, age 90, passed away peacefully on February 13, 2026, at 12:47 p.m., with her family by her side. Carol was born on April 11, 1935, in Sumner County, Kansas, to Elmer Edward Riley and Lillian Madge Arnold Riley. She was the seventh of nine children. She was baptized and confirmed at South Haven Methodist Church in South Haven, Kansas, beginning a lifelong devotion to her faith that would guide every season of her life. On March 17, 1957, she married Gottfried “Jeff” John Kolb at the Methodist Church in South Haven, Kansas. Their marriage was a testament to love, faith, and lifelong devotion. Together they built a beautiful life and were blessed with three daughters, gran

obituaries.name: Carol McGuire
obituaries.summary: Carol McGuire, 87, of Waco, TX passed away on February 20, just two days shy of her eighty-eighth birthday. The funeral service will be held at 2:00 p.m., on Wednesday, March 4, at OakCrest Funeral Home with visitation at 1:00 p.m. Private burial will be at a later date. Born Carol Sue Bertram on February 22, 1938 in Lamesa, Texas to Thomas and Montez Bertram, she was raised in Hobbs, NM, the oldest of four siblings. Carol attended college at McMurry University in Abilene, singing in the college choir, the Chanters. While home on summer break she met her future husband, Lewis, at a church ice cream social hosted by her parents. Carol and Lewis were married on August 29, 1958. During that sam

obituaries.name: Fred Guardiola
obituaries.summary: Fred Guardiola passed away peacefully on February 16, 2026 in his sleep. He was born on June 17, 1970 in Boulder City, Louisiana to Mary Ellen Rodriguez and Fred Guardiola, Sr. Fred grew up in Waco, Texas and graduated from University High School. In 1991 he met the love of his life (wife) Clara Annette Shelton. Fred worked as a chef his entire life. Fred was preceded in death by his mom, Mary Ellen Guardiola; his brother, David Mata; and his daughter, Teressa Cantu. Fred leaves to cherish his memory his wife of 35 years, Clara Annette Shelton; his father, Fred Guardiola, Sr. (Wife); his brothers, Richard Guardiola and Anthony Guardiola; one sister, Betty Jean Guardiola; his children, Laura

obituaries.name: Jorge Treviño Martínez
obituaries.summary: Jorge Treviño Martínez, born on July 12, 1964, in Sabinas Hidalgo, Nuevo León, Mexico, to Bernabe Treviño Cantù and Dolores Treviño Martínez, passed from this life leaving behind a legacy of love, strength, devotion, and faith. Jorge was a beloved husband, father, brother, uncle, and friend whose presence brought warmth and guidance to everyone who knew him. He carried himself with quiet strength and a generous heart, always willing to lend a helping hand or offer words of encouragement. His love for his family was the foundation of his life, and he took great pride in caring for and providing for those he loved. Born and raised in Sabinas Hidalgo, Jorge carried with him the values of hard w

obituaries.name: Jubentino Espinoza Rodriguez
obituaries.summary: Jubentino Rodriguez, born January 1, 1942, passed away peacefully on February 16, 2026, surrounded by the deep love of his family. Jubentino was a loving and devoted husband, a proud and selfless father, and a deeply cherished grandfather. He was the constant foundation of his family, a man of unwavering faith, enduring sacrifice, and remarkable humility. His life was defined by tireless dedication and a steadfast commitment to those he loved most. He is survived by his beloved wife, Sara Rodriguez, his lifelong partner and companion, who stood beside him through every season of life with loyalty and love. He was a proud and devoted father to: Gumaro Rodriguez, Sr.; Gerardo Rodriguez and wif

obituaries.name: Pete R. Salazar, Sr.
obituaries.summary: Pete Salazar, 85 of Waco, Texas, passed away on Sunday, February 8, 2026, surrounded by his loving family. Visitation will be on Thursday, February 12 at OakCrest Funeral Home from 6-8 pm. Funeral Mass will be at 10 a.m. at Sacred Heart Catholic Church on Friday, February 13, and the burial will follow at Oakwood Cemetery. Pete was born on December 4, 1940, to Carlos Salazar and Henrietta Romero Salazar in Chilton, Texas. He married Guadalupe (Lupe) Rodriguez on February 28, 1960, who preceded him in death in 2008. Also preceding him in death were his daughter, Connie Burns and son-in-law, Kenneth Harper in 2021. Pete was a loving husband, father, grandfather (papa to his grandchildren), and

obituaries.name: Rebecca Jane Weatherly
obituaries.summary: Rebecca Jane Weatherly, 74, passed away on Thursday, February 12, 2026. Born on August 14, 1951, in Lubbock, Texas, to Bill and Ruth Kennedy, Becky spent her life deeply rooted in Texas. She was preceded in death by her parents, Bill and Ruth Kennedy, and by her beloved husband, Chris Weatherly, who passed away on September 5, 2014. A graduate of the University of Texas, Becky carried a rare combination of strength, warmth, and wit. She and Chris shared a marriage marked by devotion to Christ and a deep commitment to each other and to discipling others. Together, they walked closely with countless couples, pouring wisdom, prayer, and encouragement into marriages and families that will contin

obituaries.name: Richard Nagengast
obituaries.summary: Enter your phone number above to have directions sent via text. Standard text messaging rates apply.

obituaries.name: Stacia Bounds Daniels
obituaries.summary: Stacia (Stacy) Daniels passed away on February 8th at Providence Hospital after a brief illness. Officiating will be (Chip) George Hill Bounds, III. Services will be on February 23rd in Waco, TX at OakCrest Funeral Home. Viewing will be from 9-10am. Funeral Service will begin at 10am. Graveside services will be at 12:30pm at Fort Parker Cemetery in Grosbeck, TX. Meeting for dinner afterwards at The Ole Gin in Wortham, TX. Stacia was born in Wortham, TX. She was the youngest of four children: George Hill Bounds Jr., Lee Lavaughn Bounds, and LaSonda Varnnum nee Bounds, all born to George Hill Bounds, Sr. and Ruby Virginia Lee Johnson. Stacia married Darwin L. Daniels in 1960 in Sierra Blanca,

obituaries.name: Tracie Ann Eisenman
obituaries.summary: Tracie Ann Eisenman, 65, of Waco, passed away on February 10, 2026 at her home surrounded by her loved ones after a hard battle with cancer. Visitation will be held at 2 p.m., Wednesday, February 18, with services immediately to follow at 3 p.m. at OakCrest Funeral Home, 4520 Bosque Boulevard, Waco, TX. Tracie was born June 16, 1960, in Memphis, TN to her mother, Bobbie Holobaugh, and father, Richard (Buddy) Holobaugh. A few years after Tracie was born, her family relocated to the Waco area. Tracie graduated high school from Connally and then began working at TSTC. Tracie also worked with her sister, Wendy, in the real estate business for a brief time. Ultimately, she returned to TSTC until

obituaries.name: Bettie Jean Reaves
obituaries.summary: Bettie Jean Reaves February 16, 1931 — February 24, 2026 Hewitt Bettie Jean Reaves, 95, of Waco, Texas, passed away peacefully on February 24, 2026, just days after celebrating her birthday. Bettie was born to Sherman and Edith (Mackie) Mills in Waco, Texas. She grew up in South Waco near the 15th Street area and later moved to Rosenthal, Texas, where she attended school before graduating from Waco High School. Bettie was a long-time member of Hewitt First Baptist Church. In 1950, Bettie married Arno Frosch, and together they welcomed two children, David and Sherrie. In 1961, she met and married Virgil “Bobby” Reaves, gaining two more children, Patsy and Mike. Bettie cherished her role as a

obituaries.name: C. Lois Marchbanks
obituaries.summary: C. Lois Marchbanks October 19, 1961 — February 17, 2026 Waco C. Lois (Harris) Marchbanks, age 64, of Waco, peacefully passed away Tuesday, February 17, 2026 surrounded by her family. There will be a visitation with viewing from 3:00-5:00 p.m. Sunday, February 22nd at Pecan Grove Funeral Home. Lois was born October 19, 1961 to Lloyd and Inez (Chisum) Harris in Waco, Texas. She married William ‘Mike’ Marchbanks on November 20, 1981, sharing a content life of almost 45 years together. She enjoyed crossword puzzles, crafting projects, homemaking, and spending time with her daughters. She was preceded in death by her parents, Lloyd and Inez Harris; brother, Troy Riggs; and sister, Mattie Lefever.

obituaries.name: Ernestine Puente
obituaries.summary: Ernestine Puente March 16, 1934 — February 7, 2026 Waco Ernestine Puente, 91, of Waco, passed away on February 7, 2026 in Waco. She was born in Buda, TX to Concepcion Suares on March 16, 1934. She was married to Moises H. Puente Sr. of 52 years. She worked as a tech for Marathon Battery for many years. Ernestine was preceded in death by her husband Moises Hernandez Puente Sr, Daughter-in-law Cindy Sintia Morales Puente, and two sons, Roland Puente and Eric Puente. Survivors include son Moises Hernandez Puente Jr: granddaughter Melissa Meetze & her husband Phillip Meetze, grandson Shawn Puente Sr. and his wife Kelly Puente, granddaughter Monique Casarez, granddaughter Mariah Ellis and her hus

obituaries.name: J. Lester Taylor Sr.
obituaries.summary: J. Lester Taylor Sr. February 15, 1930 — February 13, 2026 Robinson J. Lester Taylor, Sr., age 95, of Robinson, Texas passed away Friday, February 13, 2026. A celebration of life will be held at 2:00 p.m. Sunday, March 1st at Lee Lockwood Library and Museum, 2801 W. Waco Drive, Waco, Texas with Pastor David Cozart, Executive Pastor Meadowbrook Baptist Church officiating and Mike Waldrop presiding over the Masonic Memorial Service. There will be a visitation one hour prior to the service beginning at 1:00 p.m. J. Lester Taylor was born February 15, 1930, to William Bryan Sr. and Margie (Smalley) Taylor in Waco, Texas. Lester was a lifelong Baptist and member of Meadowbrook Baptist Church in W

obituaries.name: James Robert Cox
obituaries.summary: James Robert Cox August 12, 1935 — February 19, 2026 Robinson James Robert “Bob” Cox , age 90, passed away in Waco, Texas on February 19, 2026 to be with his Lord and Savior. He was born in Jacksonville, Texas on August 12, 1935 to Larue and Emma Lena McCorkle Cox and lived in East Texas during his childhood years. He moved to Denton, Texas in 1952 to attend North Texas State College. After receiving his Bachelor of Science degree in 1955 he was inducted into the U. S. Army, serving two years as a medical corpsman in the Medical Detachment, Headquarters Company, 32nd Tank Battalion, 3rd Armored Division. In his first year of duty he served at Fort Knox, Kentucky and the following year he was

obituaries.name: Johnny Joe Gomez
obituaries.summary: Johnny Joe Gomez August 1, 1953 — February 18, 2026 Robinson Johnny Joe Gomez, 72, of Robinson, Texas passed away on February 18, 2026. He was born on August 1, 1953 in McGregor, Texas. Johnny went to school in Ogelsby. He enjoyed fishing and was a truck driver for twenty - five plus years. His hobby included a collection of baseball and football cards. He was a veteran and served in the United States Marine Corps from 1975 to 1979. Johnny is preceded in death by his wife, Vicki Vasquez Gomez and his mom and dad, Carmen and Fred Gonzales. Survivors include his daughters, Irene, Crystal, Cindy and husband Jason, and Lindy; grandchildren, Alex and wife Bri, Maribelle, Chloe, Meagan, and Reagan

obituaries.name: Lance Tyler Tosh
obituaries.summary: Lance Tyler Tosh June 13, 1990 — February 10, 2026 Waco Lance Tyler Tosh, 35, of Waco, TX, passed away Tuesday, February 10, 2026. Lance was born in Waco, TX, to David Tosh and Kim (Brietenstein) Tosh. He was a graduate of Midway High School. Lance enjoyed working in restaurants around Waco and was an excellent cook. He loved animals, and recently adopted his newest puppy, Zendaya. Lance was preceded in death by his mother, Kim Ilene Tosh. He is survived by his father David Tosh and wife Lori; sister, Leah Stow and husband Tommy; brother, Hunter Tosh; nephews Jackson Stow and Wesley Stow; niece, Lyric Stow; uncle, Kenny Tosh and wife Terri; grandmother, Marlene McDow; grandfather, William To

obituaries.name: Larry Clinton Jackson Obituary February 4, 2026 - Pecan Grove Funeral Home
obituaries.summary: Larry Clinton Jackson, a beloved husband, father, grandfather, and friend, departed this world on February 4, 2026, in Waco, Texas, at the age of 78. Born on June 22, 1947, in Fort Worth, Texas, Larry's journey through life was one of hard work, devotion to family, and a quiet strength that was felt by all who knew him.

obituaries.name: Reba June Saulters
obituaries.summary: Reba June Saulters October 5, 1942 — February 19, 2026 Robisnon Reba June Saulters, 83, of Robinson, TX, passed away Thursday, February 19, 2026, at home, with her family by her side. Graveside services will be 11:00 a.m., Thursday, February 26, at Robinson Cemetery, with Brother Robert Nelson officiating. June was born in Sterling, TX, to Archie and Ruth (Fowler) Gentry. She worked as a realtor in her family’s real estate company for many years and she and her husband, Phillip, were owners of Hi-tech Kennels. She found great joy in working with Phillip, whether around the house or simply spending time together outdoors. Her greatest happiness was found in caring for her family and gathering

obituaries.name: Wendy Lynn Winter
obituaries.summary: Wendy Lynn Winter October 14, 1974 — February 14, 2026 Robinson Wendy Lynn (Reinke) Winter, age 51, of Robinson, Texas, passed away peacefully on Saturday, February 14, 2026. Wendy was born on October 14, 1974, in Waco, Texas, to Leonard and Diana (Kelinske) Reinke. She grew up in the Waco area and was a graduate of Bosqueville High School. On August 1, 2004, Wendy married the love of her life, Michael Ray Winter. Together they shared 21 years of marriage and built a life centered on love, family, and devotion to their children. She was preceded in death by her mother, Diana Reinke, and her brother, Jay Reinke. Wendy is survived by her loving husband, Michael Winter; her son, Jacob Winter; h

obituaries.name: Barbara Jane Wilks
obituaries.summary: Barbara Jane Wilks, born on January 6, 1940, in Valley Mills, Texas, passed away on February 4, 2026, in Waco, Texas. A beloved mother, grandmother, and friend, Barbara's journey through life was marked by her vibrant spirit and caring nature.

obituaries.name: Benny Joe Jones Obituary January 18, 2026 - Waco Memorial Funeral Home
obituaries.summary: Benny Joe Jones, age 89, of Waco, Texas, passed away peacefully Sunday, January 18, 2026, at his daughter’s home in Waco. A visitation will be held at Waco Memorial Funeral Home Thursday, January 22, 2026, from 6:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m., Funeral Services will be 11:00 a.m. Friday, January 23, 2026, at Memorial Funeral Home Chapel.

obituaries.name: Bernadine Ruth Watson
obituaries.summary: Bernadine Ruth Watson February 27, 1928 — February 4, 2026 Waco Bernadine Ruth Watson of Waco, Texas passed away peacefully on February 4, 2026, just 23 days short of her 98th birthday. She was born on February 27, 1928 on a farm just outside of Brenham, Texas, the second child of Herbert and Erna Gummelt. Bernadine and her family moved to Waco as her father searched for work during the Great Depression. She attended and graduated from Waco High School where she met her eventual husband, Foy Wallace Watson, Jr. They were married on April 21, 1947 following his return from Pacific service with the US Navy in World War II. They were married for 63 years until his death in 2010. Following the b

obituaries.name: Cecil Sanchez Day, Sr.
obituaries.summary: Cecil Sanchez Day, Sr., 55, of Dallas, Texas, passed away peacefully at home, surrounded by love, on Wednesday, January 14, 2026. Services are scheduled for Tuesday, January 20, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. at Waco Memorial Funeral Home. Interment will follow at Waco Memorial Park. Cecil was born on June 9, 1970, in Waco, Texas, to Albert and Santos Day. After moving to Dallas, he attended South Grand Prairie High School, where his outgoing nature and quick sense of humor made him well known. A natural class clown, Cecil loved making people laugh and had an unforgettable way of lighting up any room. Cecil had a love for music and was a great dancer who enjoyed good company. Family was the center of C

obituaries.name: Everett Wayne Speas
obituaries.summary: Everett Wayne Speas January 9, 1937 — January 26, 2026 Hewitt Everett Wayne Speas passed away on January 26, 2026. Visitation will be Monday, February 2, 2026, from 6:00 pm to 8:00 pm at Waco Memorial Funeral Home. His service will be Tuesday, February 3, 2026, at 1:00 pm at the funeral home with Pastor Wayne Williams officiating. Interment will follow at Waco Memorial Park. Everett was born to Walter & Esther Speas at home in Cazenovia, Illinois on January 9,1937. Everett was the second child and first-born son of their four children. After graduating high school in 1955 he joined the Navy and served as an Airborne Early Warning Typhoon Tracker. He served in the Hawaiian Islands, Guam and O

obituaries.name: Terry Lee Ramsey
obituaries.summary: Terry Lee Ramsey, 65, of Allen, Texas passed away peacefully on February 6, 2026, surrounded by his family. Terry was born June 22, 1960, in Waco, the fourth child of Charlie and JoAnn (Kutzenberger) Ramsey. Terry accepted Christ at the age of eleven. He was raised in Waco and graduated from Waco High School. Terry married Mary Ellen on December 17, 2023, in Waco at his father and stepmother’s church. Terry was always ready for a new adventure and lived his life full of excitement, energy, and friendliness. He and Mary Ellen enjoyed many trips on his motorcycle. Terry had a close relationship with his father and was close to his family and friends. Terry was preceded in death by his mother,

obituaries.name: Tyra Lou (Tacker) Lewis
obituaries.summary: At the sweet age of 77 Tyra Lou Lewis went home to be with the Lord on Friday, January 16th with loved ones by her side. Visitation will be Thursday, January 22, 2026 from 9am to 11am at Waco Memorial Funeral Home. Services will begin at 11am at the funeral home with interment to follow at Oakwood Cemetery.

obituaries.name: Tyrone Svehla Obituary January 25, 2026 - Waco Memorial Funeral Home
obituaries.summary: Tyrone Svehla, a proud veteran and devoted family man, passed away on January 25, 2026, in Hewitt, Texas. He was 74 years old.

obituaries.name: Wayne Wells Obituary January 24, 2026 - Waco Memorial Funeral Home
obituaries.summary: Wayne Douglas Wells was born on May 31, 1935 in Valley Mills, Texas, and passed away on Saturday, January 24, 2026, at Wesley Woods Rehabilitation and Care Center. A service to celebrate his life will be Thursday, January 29, 2026 at 12:30pm at Waco Memorial Funeral Home.

obituaries.name: Antonio Villaneuva, Jr.
obituaries.summary: Antonio Villaneuva, Jr. October 29, 1956 — February 15, 2026 Waco Antonio Villanueva Jr., 69, passed away in Grandview, Texas, leaving behind a legacy of hard work, generosity, and deep devotion to his family and friends. A memorial service to celebrate Tony’s life will be held at 2:00 p.m., Saturday, February 28, 2026, at the Living Witness Missionary Baptist Church in Waco, Texas. The service will be officiated by the Reverend Maurice Walker. Born on October 29, 1956, in Waco, Texas, Antonio, affectionately known as Tony, was the son of Antonio Villanueva, Sr. and Beatrice Mansolo Villanueva. He was the third of eight children and grew up surrounded by a large, loving family. From a young

obituaries.name: Donald "Duby" Hurst
obituaries.summary: Donald "Duby" Hurst January 2, 1932 — February 17, 2026 Waco Donald “Duby” Hurst, 94, passed away Tuesday, February 17, 2026, leaving behind a legacy of faith, devotion, and steadfast love. His Memorial Service will be held at First Baptist Lorena Saturday, February 21st at 1:00. Born January 2, 1932, in Lorena, Texas, Donald carried the values of his hometown throughout his life. He proudly served in the U.S. Army as a tank commander during the Korean War, demonstrating the courage and quiet strength that defined him. After his service, Donald attended Baylor University, where he met his beloved wife, Sybil Nell Townsend. Together they built a life rooted in faith, family, and service—first

obituaries.name: Jane Ellen Johnson
obituaries.summary: Jane Ellen Johnson April 2, 1945 — February 16, 2026 Waco Jane Ellen (Barnett) Johnson, 80, passed away Monday, February 16, 2026, peacefully in her sleep in Siloam Springs, Arkansas. The visitation will be from 4:00 p.m. until 6:00 p.m., Sunday, February 22, at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home, 6101 Bosque Blvd, Waco. Graveside will be at 10:00 am on Monday, February 23, at Oakwood Cemetery, followed by the funeral service at 11:00 am at the First Baptist Church of Waco, 500 Webster Ave. A catered meal will follow. She was born on April 2, 1945, in Siloam Springs, Arkansas, to Ray and Laurine Barnett, the 2nd of their 7 children and their first daughter. Her father was stationed in the U

obituaries.name: Joe Ray Sudderth
obituaries.summary: Joe Ray Sudderth January 18, 1937 — February 13, 2026 Waco Joe Ray Sudderth, 89, of Waco, passed away peacefully Friday, February 13, 2026. Visitation will be from 5:00 p.m.-7:00 p.m. Wednesday evening, February 18, at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home. The funeral service will be held at 1:00 p.m., Thursday, February 19, at First Presbyterian Church, 1100 Austin Avenue in Waco, with Rev. Dr. Leslie King officiating. Burial will follow at Oakwood Cemetery. Our beloved and loving husband, father, grandfather, and great-grandfather, lived in Waco, Texas for over 50 years with his devoted wife, Amelia, until her passing in 2022. Born and raised in Sherman, Texas, Joe graduated from Austin Col

obituaries.name: Juanita Melendez
obituaries.summary: Juanita Melendez May 14, 1937 — February 21, 2026 Waco Juanita Melendez, age 88, of Waco, Texas, entered into eternal rest at her home on February 21, 2026, and went to be with her Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ. A viewing will be held at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home in Waco on Friday, February 27, 2026, from 5:00 p.m. until 7:00 p.m. A celebration of Janie’s life will be held the next morning at 10:00 a.m. on Saturday, February 28, in the chapel at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey. Burial will follow at Oakwood Cemetery, with Pastor Pete Arvizu presiding. In lieu of flowers, donations may be made in honor of Juanita Melendez to a local animal shelter or rescue organization of your choice—a caus

obituaries.name: Julie "Dewey" Claire LeBlanc
obituaries.summary: Julie "Dewey" Claire LeBlanc October 31, 1931 — January 25, 2026 Waco Julie “Dewey” Claire LeBlanc passed away peacefully on January 25, 2026. Julie was born on October 31, 1931, to Anna Ruth (Duffy) and Julius Rodney Leblanc in Waco, TX. She graduated from Baylor University where she received master’s degrees in education and music. Julie was a devoted educator for over 40 years. She taught at various Waco schools, but most of her career was spent shaping the young minds at Le Vega ISD. As a devout Catholic, she was an active member she was an active member of Saint Mary’s Catholic Church, then later at Saint Louis Catholic Church. She had a giving heart and enjoyed supporting many charitie

obituaries.name: Linda Kay Berry
obituaries.summary: Linda Kay Berry July 17, 1942 — February 20, 2026 Waco Linda “Kay” Berry, 83, of Waco, passed away peacefully Friday, February 20, 2026, in Waco. A celebration of her life will be held at 12:30 p.m. Saturday, February 28, at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home with Pastor Roy Marshall officiating. Kay was born to Norman Russell and Mary Elliston Berry on July 17, 1942, in Seagraves, TX. After the family moved to Abbott, Texas, she graduated as the Salutatorian of Abbott High School and began her university studies at Baylor University. After graduating with her bachelor’s degree, she continued her education and earned a master’s degree in social work from Louisiana Tech University. She began

obituaries.name: Onetia Sheppard
obituaries.summary: Onetia Sheppard April 7, 1936 — February 19, 2026 Waco Onetia Sheppard of Woodway passed away peacefully Thursday, February 19, 2026, at her home. The graveside service will be 10:00 a.m., Wednesday, February 25, 2026, at Oakwood Cemetery, 2124 S. 5th St., Waco, TX. Visitation will be on Tuesday, February 24, from 5-7 p.m., at Wilkirson-Hatch-Bailey Funeral Home, 6101 Bosque Blvd., Waco, TX. Onetia was a faithful follower and servant of Jesus Christ during her entire adult life. She was dearly known and counted on as a devoted prayer warrior and faithful friend to many. She will leave a lasting legacy and impact on many lives. Onetia was born April 7, 1936, in Lufkin, Texas. She graduated fr

obituaries.name: Steven Daniel Clark
obituaries.summary: Steven Daniel Clark June 19, 1985 — January 19, 2026 Waco Steven Daniel Clark, 40, of Reisel, passed away in his home, Monday, January 19, 2026, of a heart attack. A Celebration of Life will be held at 12:00 p.m. Saturday, February 28, 2026, at the V.F.W. 725 Sun Valley Boulevard, Hewitt, TX 76643. Steven was born in Canton, Ohio on June 19, 1985. He was a 2004 graduate of Midway High School in Hewitt, TX. He is preceded in death by his father, Michael Ernest Clark, oldest brother, Michael William Clark, maternal grandparents, Edwin and Ruth King, and paternal grandparents, William Clark and Valerie Coffman. He is survived by his wife Cheryl Clark and amazing son Joseph, of Riesel; parents W

obituaries.name: William Rost Falco
obituaries.summary: William Rost Falco September 29, 1943 — February 19, 2026 Waco William Rost Falco (Bill) passed from this life on February 19, 2026, leaving behind a city he helped shape, a family he adored beyond measure, and a community forever better because he walked among us. A burial will be held at 10:00 a.m., Friday, March 6, 2026, at Oakwood Cemetery. A memorial service will follow at 11:00 a.m. at First Presbyterian Church of Waco. Born in Navasota, Texas, to Joe Savaggo Falco and Elvera Rost Falco, Bill’s roots were firmly planted in Texas soil.
